---
title: "caption_block"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/stwd.R#L547)

# `caption_block()`

Create a Caption Block Plot

## Usage

```r
caption_block(
  caption,
  caption_size = 6,
  halign = "left",
  color = "#808080",
  margin_top = 10,
  margin_bottom = 5,
  margin_left = 5,
  margin_right = 5,
  wrap_width = NULL,
  ...
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `caption` | Caption text (marquee-formatted). |
| `caption_size` | Size for caption in pts. Default: 6. |
| `halign` | Horizontal alignment. Default: "left". |
| `color` | Text color. Default: gray (#808080). |
| `margin_top` | Top margin in pts. Default: 10. |
| `margin_bottom` | Bottom margin in pts. Default: 5. |
| `margin_left` | Left margin in pts. Default: 5. |
| `margin_right` | Right margin in pts. Default: 5. |
| `wrap_width` | Character width to wrap text at. Default: NULL (no wrapping). |
| `...` | Additional arguments passed to `marquee::geom_marquee()`. |

## Value

A ggplot object that can be combined with other plots via patchwork.

## Description

Creates a standalone caption element as a ggplot for use at the
bottom of patchwork compositions.

## Details

Captions typically contain source attributions, methodology notes, or
data caveats. Displayed in muted gray by default to avoid competing
with the main content. Supports marquee markdown formatting.

Place at the bottom using patchwork's `/` operator.

## Examples

```r
## Not run: 
library(patchwork)

caption_plot <- caption_block("Source: Internal Sales Database")

my_chart / caption_plot + plot_layout(heights = c(0.95, 0.05))

## End(Not run)
```
